context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the review-architecture instructions from the published Rails Agent Skills tile.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use this skill when the task is to review or improve the structure of a Rails application or library.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Core principle:** Prioritize boundary problems over style. Prefer simple objects and explicit flow over hidden behavior.

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Check where domain logic lives.

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Check whether abstractions clarify the design or only move code around.

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Verify each High-severity finding** by reading the actual code — confirm it is a real structural problem, not just a pattern match on file size or line count. If verification reveals the finding is not a genuine structural problem, either downgrade it to Medium/Low with a revised rationale, or remove it entirely. Do not list findings that do not survive code-level confirmation.
